presetSummary.focusArea: Wide or zone area with tracking.
presetSummary.whiteBalance: Daylight for sun, Cloudy/Shade for overcast; use custom WB when color accuracy matters more than speed.
presetSummary.flashGuidance: Stay at or below 1/250 for conventional flash sync unless you intentionally move into HSS.
presetSummary.lensFit: The selected lens range fits this session well.

qualityControls.colorAccuracy: If color consistency matters across a set, avoid letting Auto WB drift from frame to frame.
qualityControls.repeatability: When building a series, change one variable at a time so you can trace what improved or degraded the result.
qualityControls.hiddenChoiceAwareness: Exposure, focal length, viewpoint, processing, and tone mapping all change the meaning of the image. Treat them as deliberate decisions rather than invisible defaults.

composition.placement: Default to slightly off-center placement unless the face or object is intentionally formal, frontal, or symmetric.
composition.ruleBreaking: Treat composition rules as priors, not laws. Break them when the frame gains clarity, balance, or emphasis.
composition.backgroundStrategy: Reduce background clutter and hue count. Cleaner separation and selective blur usually improve perceived quality when the subject is the story.
composition.depthOfFieldUse: Use depth of field as a subject-isolation tool, not a default aesthetic. Blur helps when it removes distractions without sacrificing critical detail.

checklist: Set the camera to Av with RAW capture and highlight warnings enabled.
Use evaluative metering and confirm exposure from the histogram before the session starts.
Confirm single autofocus behavior and your focus area before the key moment begins.
Check card space, battery status, and front-element cleanliness before shooting.
Use full-resolution capture for the master files and create smaller derivatives only after review.
Keep flash work at or below 1/250 unless you intentionally switch to HSS.
Use the strengths of Canon RF 24-70mm f/2.8L IS USM instead of forcing it into a role it does not naturally cover.
caveats: Shutter 1/400 exceeds flash sync 1/250. Use M mode or HSS flash.
scienceNotes: A static handheld starting floor with this lens is about 1/80 before stabilization or subject motion changes the equation.
The best available stabilization gives roughly 8.5 stops of buffer, so static subjects may hold around 4s if your technique is clean.
On full-frame, f/16 and smaller apertures are a depth-of-field tool, not a default choice, because diffraction builds quickly there too.
Photo-quality studies consistently reward clearer subject isolation, simpler backgrounds, and lower color clutter when the image depends on one primary subject.

lensName: Sony FE 70-200mm f/2.8 GM OSS II

presetSummary.focusArea: Wide or zone area with tracking.
presetSummary.whiteBalance: Tungsten/Auto or roughly 3000-4200K depending on how warm you want the scene.
presetSummary.flashGuidance: Stay at or below 1/400 for conventional flash sync unless you intentionally move into HSS.
presetSummary.lensFit: The selected lens range fits this session well.

qualityControls.colorAccuracy: Mixed lighting makes perfect color control unrealistic. Prioritize skin or subject color over background lighting gimmicks.
qualityControls.repeatability: When building a series, change one variable at a time so you can trace what improved or degraded the result.
qualityControls.hiddenChoiceAwareness: Exposure, focal length, viewpoint, processing, and tone mapping all change the meaning of the image. Treat them as deliberate decisions rather than invisible defaults.

composition.placement: Rule-of-thirds placement is a good starting point, but do not force it if the scene becomes weaker.
composition.ruleBreaking: Treat composition rules as priors, not laws. Break them when the frame gains clarity, balance, or emphasis.
composition.backgroundStrategy: Keep the background intentional. Every bright edge or extra color competes with the main subject.
composition.depthOfFieldUse: Choose depth of field based on what must stay legible in the frame, not because a wide aperture is automatically better.

presetSummary.focusArea: Use the smallest area that stays fast enough for the subject.
presetSummary.whiteBalance: Daylight for sun, Cloudy/Shade for overcast; use custom WB when color accuracy matters more than speed.

qualityControls.colorAccuracy: If color consistency matters across a set, avoid letting Auto WB drift from frame to frame.

qualityControls.hiddenChoiceAwareness: Exposure, focal length, viewpoint, processing, and tone mapping all change the meaning of the image. Treat them as deliberate decisions rather than invisible defaults.

composition.placement: Start with thirds or an offset horizon, but let strong leading lines or a vanishing point dictate the final placement if they read more clearly.
composition.ruleBreaking: Treat composition rules as priors, not laws. Break them when the frame gains clarity, balance, or emphasis.
composition.backgroundStrategy: Use layering, scale, and directional lines before relying on shallow depth of field to create depth.
composition.depthOfFieldUse: Bias toward enough depth of field to preserve informative detail. Add blur only when it serves the story more than the information.

lensName: Sony FE 90mm f/2.8 Macro G OSS

presetSummary.focusArea: Single point or small flexible spot for precise placement.
presetSummary.whiteBalance: Daylight for sun, Cloudy/Shade for overcast; use custom WB when color accuracy matters more than speed.
presetSummary.flashGuidance: Stay at or below 1/250 for conventional flash sync unless you intentionally move into HSS.
presetSummary.lensFit: The selected lens range fits this session well.

qualityControls.colorAccuracy: For color-critical work, lock white balance and record a neutral gray or color target whenever the lighting setup changes.
qualityControls.repeatability: For repeatable deliverable sets, keep focal length, camera height, camera-to-subject distance, background, aperture, exposure, and white balance fixed across the sequence.
qualityControls.hiddenChoiceAwareness: Exposure, focal length, viewpoint, processing, and tone mapping all change the meaning of the image. Treat them as deliberate decisions rather than invisible defaults.

composition.placement: Default to slightly off-center placement unless the face or object is intentionally formal, frontal, or symmetric.
composition.ruleBreaking: Treat composition rules as priors, not laws. Break them when the frame gains clarity, balance, or emphasis.
composition.backgroundStrategy: Reduce background clutter and hue count. Cleaner separation and selective blur usually improve perceived quality when the subject is the story.
composition.depthOfFieldUse: Use depth of field as a subject-isolation tool, not a default aesthetic. Blur helps when it removes distractions without sacrificing critical detail.

checklist: Set the camera to M with RAW capture and highlight warnings enabled.
Use evaluative metering and confirm exposure from the histogram before the session starts.
Confirm single autofocus behavior and your focus area before the key moment begins.
Check card space, battery status, and front-element cleanliness before shooting.
Use a timer or remote release and turn stabilization off only if tripod-induced drift appears.
Photograph a gray card or color target when the lighting setup changes and lock white balance for the actual series.
Lock camera position, focal length, and background before shooting the sequence so the set stays comparable frame to frame.
Use full-resolution capture for the master files and create smaller derivatives only after review.
Keep flash work at or below 1/250 unless you intentionally switch to HSS.
Use the strengths of Sony FE 90mm f/2.8 Macro G OSS instead of forcing it into a role it does not naturally cover.
caveats: Auto white balance, auto exposure drift, and casual recomposition can quietly destroy cross-frame consistency in a deliverable series.
scienceNotes: A static handheld starting floor with this lens is about 1/100 before stabilization or subject motion changes the equation.
The best available stabilization gives roughly 7.5 stops of buffer, so static subjects may hold around 2s if your technique is clean.
On full-frame, f/16 and smaller apertures are a depth-of-field tool, not a default choice, because diffraction builds quickly there too.
Photo-quality studies consistently reward clearer subject isolation, simpler backgrounds, and lower color clutter when the image depends on one primary subject.

checklist: Set the camera to M with RAW capture and highlight warnings enabled.
Use spot metering and confirm exposure from the histogram before the session starts.
Confirm continuous autofocus behavior and your focus area before the key moment begins.
Check card space, battery status, and front-element cleanliness before shooting.
Enable anti-flicker or variable shutter if the venue is LED-heavy.
Pre-focus, keep both eyes open, and start tracking before the peak action enters the frame.
Use full-resolution capture for the master files and create smaller derivatives only after review.
Keep flash work at or below 1/250 unless you intentionally switch to HSS.
Use the strengths of Sony FE 24-70mm f/2.8 GM II instead of forcing it into a role it does not naturally cover.
caveats: Indoor LED and discharge lighting can break otherwise good-looking presets; always test for banding at the real venue.
scienceNotes: A static handheld starting floor with this lens is about 1/80 before stabilization or subject motion changes the equation.
The best available stabilization gives roughly 7.5 stops of buffer, so static subjects may hold around 2s if your technique is clean.
Stabilization does not freeze subject motion; once people, animals, or vehicles move, shutter speed matters more than IBIS or OIS.
On full-frame, f/16 and smaller apertures are a depth-of-field tool, not a default choice, because diffraction builds quickly there too.

checklist: Set the camera to M with RAW capture and highlight warnings enabled.
Use evaluative metering and confirm exposure from the histogram before the session starts.
Confirm manual autofocus behavior and your focus area before the key moment begins.
Check card space, battery status, and front-element cleanliness before shooting.
Use a timer or remote release and turn stabilization off only if tripod-induced drift appears.
Disable long-exposure NR if cadence matters and shoot a separate dark frame set instead.
Use full-resolution capture for the master files and create smaller derivatives only after review.
Keep flash work at or below 1/250 unless you intentionally switch to HSS.
Use the strengths of Sony FE 20mm f/1.8 G instead of forcing it into a role it does not naturally cover.
scienceNotes: A static handheld starting floor with this lens is about 1/20 before stabilization or subject motion changes the equation.
The best available stabilization gives roughly 7.5 stops of buffer, so static subjects may hold around 4s if your technique is clean.
For untracked astro, a simple 500-rule start point is about 25s at the wide end before star trailing becomes obvious.
On full-frame, f/16 and smaller apertures are a depth-of-field tool, not a default choice, because diffraction builds quickly there too.
